description: Printing progress, warnings, or errors to stdout so they contaminate the data consumed by the next command in a pipe.
body: Failure mode: a tool writes status messages, progress bars, or error text to stdout alongside its real output, so `tool | parser` feeds the parser garbage interleaved with data. Detection signals: piping the tool breaks the downstream parser; `tool > file` produces a file with log lines mixed into data; errors disappear when stdout is redirected. Do not send diagnostics to stdout. Safer fallback: route only the result payload to stdout and all status/warnings/errors to stderr, then verify with redirection that stdout holds clean data only.
